vocabulary.english: To be windy / Wind is blowing

vocabulary.pinyin: shén me shí hou huí lái

vocabulary.english: Got used to it / Accustomed now

key_sentences.pinyin: Tā shén me shí hou huí lái?
Tā dà yuē sān diǎn zuǒ yòu huí lái.
key_sentences.english: He'll come back at around three o'clock.

key_sentences.chinese: 今天刮风，差不多五度左右。
key_sentences.pinyin: Jīn tiān guā fēng, chà bu duō wǔ dù zuǒ yòu.
key_sentences.english: It's windy today, about five degrees or so.

key_sentences.chinese: 他总是这么早出去，我已经习惯了。
key_sentences.pinyin: Tā zǒng shì zhè me zǎo chū qù, wǒ yǐ jīng xí guàn le.
key_sentences.english: He always goes out this early; I'm already used to it.

key_sentences.chinese: 你愿意帮我等他回来吗？
key_sentences.pinyin: Nǐ yuàn yì bāng wǒ děng tā huí lái ma?
key_sentences.english: Are you willing to help me wait for him to come back?

key_sentences.chinese: 行，请进来吧，外面风太大了。
key_sentences.pinyin: Xíng, qǐng jìn lái ba, wài miàn fēng tài dà le.
key_sentences.english: OK, come on in; the wind outside is too strong.

grammar_points.title: 把字句（一）— The Bǎ-Sentence Part 1
grammar_points.explanation: 把 moves the object before the verb to emphasise what happened to it. Formula: Subject + 把 + Object + Verb + Result/Direction Complement. Used when the verb has a result or direction complement attached. E.g. 把门关上 = close the door (emphasis: the door is now closed). 把书放好 = put the book away properly. This structure cannot be used with simple verbs alone — there must be a result or complement.

examples.pinyin: Tā bǎ shū fàng hǎo le.
examples.english: He put the book away properly.

exercises.question: What does the 把 structure emphasise?
How do you say 'Close the window' using 把?
What must come after the verb in a 把 sentence?
What does 把门关上 mean?

grammar_points.title: 表示「大约」的词语 — Words for Approximate Numbers
grammar_points.explanation: Chinese has several words for approximation. 左右 comes AFTER a number/time: 三点左右 (around 3 o'clock), 十个左右 (about ten). 大约 and 差不多 come BEFORE: 大约三点, 差不多三点. 大约 is more formal; 差不多 also means 'almost the same'. You can combine: 大约三点左右 (though slightly redundant, it's used in speech).

examples.pinyin: Tā dà yuē wǔ fēn zhōng hòu huí lái.
examples.english: He'll be back in about five minutes.

examples.pinyin: Sān diǎn zuǒ yòu tā jiù chū qù le.
examples.english: He went out at around three o'clock.

exercises.question: Where does 左右 go relative to the number?
options: At the start of the sentence

exercises.question: How do you say 'About two o'clock'?
option_pinyin: dà yuē zuǒ yòu liǎng diǎn

exercises.question: Which word also means 'almost the same' in addition to 'approximately'?
Fill in: 他___三点___回来。(He came back at around three.)
Which sentence correctly uses an approximation word?

grammar_points.title: 总是 vs 一直 — Always vs. All Along
grammar_points.explanation: 总是 (zǒng shì) means 'always' — a habitual or repeated action. 一直 (yī zhí) means 'continuously / all along' — an uninterrupted action or state. 他总是迟到 = He always comes late (habit). 他一直等我 = He waited for me all along (without stopping). 总是 focuses on repetition; 一直 focuses on continuity.

examples.pinyin: Tā zǒng shì zhè me zǎo chū qù.
examples.english: He always goes out this early.

examples.pinyin: Tā yī zhí zài děng tā huí lái.
examples.english: She has been waiting all along for him to come back.

exercises.question: Which word expresses a habitual/repeated action?
What does 他一直等我 mean?
exercises.correct: He waited for me all along (without stopping)
options: He waited for me all along (without stopping)
He came back to wait for me

exercises.question: Fill in: 他___迟到，我已经___他了。(He always comes late; I've gotten used to him [now].)
Which sentence means 'She is always willing to help'?
Which word focuses on continuity rather than repetition?

quiz.question: What does 刮风 mean?
quiz.correct: To be windy / Wind is blowing
options: To be windy / Wind is blowing

quiz.question: What does 行 mean in conversation?
Where does 左右 go in a time expression?
How do you say 'When will he come back?'
What is the difference between 总是 and 一直?
quiz.correct: 总是 = habitual repetition; 一直 = continuous without stopping
options: 总是 = habitual repetition; 一直 = continuous without stopping
总是 = continuous; 一直 = habitual
They mean exactly the same thing
总是 = approximately; 一直 = always

quiz.question: Fill in: 他___三点左右___来，我一直等他。(He'll come back at around three; I've been waiting.)
What does 差不多两点 mean?
What does 愿意帮忙 mean?
What does 习惯了 mean?
quiz.correct: Got used to it / Accustomed now
options: Got used to it / Accustomed now

quiz.question: What does 出去一趟 mean?

lines.speaker: A
lines.chinese: 大明，你哥哥什么时候回来？
lines.pinyin: Dà Míng, nǐ gē ge shén me shí hou huí lái?
lines.english: Da Ming, when will your brother come back?

lines.speaker: B
lines.chinese: 他说大约三点左右，差不多快到了。
lines.pinyin: Tā shuō dà yuē sān diǎn zuǒ yòu, chà bu duō kuài dào le.
lines.english: He said around three o'clock; he should be almost here.

lines.speaker: A
lines.chinese: 他总是这么晚，你不生气吗？
lines.pinyin: Tā zǒng shì zhè me wǎn, nǐ bù shēng qì ma?
lines.english: He's always this late; aren't you annoyed?

lines.speaker: B
lines.pinyin: Méi bàn fǎ, wǒ yǐ jīng xí guàn le.
lines.english: Can't do anything about it; I'm already used to it.

lines.speaker: A
lines.chinese: 今天外面刮风，你愿意在这里等他吗？
lines.pinyin: Jīn tiān wài miàn guā fēng, nǐ yuàn yì zài zhè lǐ děng tā ma?
lines.english: It's windy outside today; are you willing to wait for him here?

lines.speaker: B
lines.chinese: 行，进来吧，我们在里面等他回来。
lines.pinyin: Xíng, jìn lái ba, wǒ men zài lǐ miàn děng tā huí lái.
lines.english: OK, come in, let's wait inside for him to come back.

dialogues.title: Asking About the Weather and Plans

lines.speaker: A
lines.chinese: 今天好冷啊，外面一直在刮风。
lines.pinyin: Jīn tiān hǎo lěng a, wài miàn yī zhí zài guā fēng.
lines.english: It's so cold today; it's been windy outside the whole time.

lines.speaker: B
lines.chinese: 是啊，风太大了。你打算出去吗？
lines.pinyin: Shì a, fēng tài dà le. Nǐ dǎ suàn chū qù ma?
lines.english: Indeed, the wind is too strong. Are you planning to go out?

lines.speaker: A
lines.chinese: 差不多下午两点我要出去一趟，大约半小时就回来。
lines.pinyin: Chà bu duō xià wǔ liǎng diǎn wǒ yào chū qù yī tàng, dà yuē bàn xiǎo shí jiù huí lái.
lines.english: At around two in the afternoon I'll go out for a bit, and be back in about half an hour.

lines.speaker: B
lines.chinese: 行，你出去的时候把门关上，外面风很大。
lines.pinyin: Xíng, nǐ chū qù de shí hou bǎ mén guān shàng, wài miàn fēng hěn dà.
lines.english: OK, when you go out close the door — the wind outside is very strong.

lines.speaker: A
lines.chinese: 好的，我总是记得关门，放心吧！
lines.pinyin: Hǎo de, wǒ zǒng shì jì de guān mén, fàng xīn ba!
lines.english: OK, I always remember to close the door — don't worry!
